item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 from a chinese furry
—^-^—
（OwO）

item.title: Memetic Virus?
item.content:encoded:
The only way I could see it going to anomalous degrees would be either that the resulting memetic effects mutate the individual into the "furry" before degrading them even further into an actual animal or something.

Something that makes it a little more frightening, while not reaching into simple mental degradation. Y'know make the symptoms be more than just mental, let's go a bit physical as well.

Otherwise, it does show the fear of something going too far. I like it. Would love to see more, but would also love to see it reach slight unrealistic horror territory. Just a suggestion.

item.title: UwU
item.content:encoded:
This was unexpected nightmare fuel.

+ show block
– hide block

When MPerry asked where Dylan was, and no one had heard from him… [0_0]

Edit: Looks like I'm safe. *sigh of relief*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We need an actually conclusion saying what happened between Perry and the MTF guy, and I think it needs to be more clearly stated that this behavior is abnormal beyond perceived by normal society. I am in a fringe aspect of the furry community and well…we don't appreciate being treated as degenerates, and this article, fictional as it is, would just affirm the belief in people. I think you need to mention eroding of perception between what is reality and fiction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rather don't appreciate how it "confirms" people with young characters as beign mentally ill or degenerates. As a furry, i can say this article needs some editing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They literally had problems with 953 (the Kumiho lady), and her apparent murder of an Agent Gallagher, well to quote: "any personnel found to have current or prior ties to the "furry" or "otaku" communities are to be immediately reassigned to other projects. - O5-██" There are likely already furries in the Foundation, so i doubt they have to specifically hire for this SCP, lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was great, it was very interesting. I enjoyed the use of the chat logs, it showed how olliefox slowly turned these people into insane and crazy versions of the furry community. I enjoy the tone and setting of this article. Plus, I'm a furry

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OwO
notices ur scp

Alright, in all seriousness, that video log was creepy AF.
+1 for that and the Obama comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crap, I'm not immune. OOF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good question. Who knows?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the number of characters in the blacked-out death tolls is correct, this SCP has managed to kill thousands of people, and those infected are eager to spread it. And they're looking at the possibility that there's something higher than stage 6, which is already the death of personality. Why is it not considered a Keter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is amazing as an article, concept, in all fields. Hands down, well deserving of a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Owo? and scp I see?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SnakAttak: pwease mistew obama im bwoke because the boss wont wet me weaw my fursuit uwu

instant +1.

item.title: Great except for one mistake.
item.content:encoded:
- Purchase of ███ animal costumes averaging at a cost of $██,███

Unrealistic for 100 to 999 fursuits to only cost $10,000 to $99,999. I've seen fursuits that inter that price range on their own.

Let me walk you through this. Basing on how it says "animal costumes" instead of fursuits, I'm assuming these are full suits. The cheapest "suit" (big air-quotes) I could find on google was this monstrosity. (technically not a fursuit but just the lowest denominator to show my point.)
Rounding that to $50, if we were to buy $100 of those it already reaches $5,000!

Now with a real fursuit. Let's be generous and say it was this one. That one being $1,000 and buying 100 would reach $100,000. So a good low.

Now a good high.😉 It could probably be from More Fur Less which can sometimes price their Full Digitgrade Suit at around $7,500 if it's super intricate like my boyfriends. (Mine only cost $5,000 because of it's simplicity.) Buying 100 of those would be $750,000. Wow!
This doesn't even count in shipping, the amount of time it would take for all of those, the fact they would probably price it up because that would backlog them on orders for a long time, taxes, and the fact that I chose the lowest possible amount they purchased.

+1 for making me do math.

Edit: Apparently my bf got it from Don't Hug Cacti, but the point still stands.

item.title: Surprisingly Good
item.content:encoded:
(btw I'm a furry)
I was about to dislike this just for looking like someone trying to shove 'furry' into this website but it's actually really well written! +1 my good, sir.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Doctor John Perez, this sort of comment is entirely uncalled for. Please refrain from making this kind of post in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
O W O

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this is absolute autism…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most likely bullets piercing his Perry's body, or him eating the MTF soldiers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Based on the premise I thought I wasn’t going to like this one, but the slow burn really paid off in the MTF Raid. Rest In Peace Dylan99, you were a man of good taste. +1

item.title: Disturbing... In a Good Way
item.content:encoded:
Well, I'm glad to know I'm immune. OWO forever, man.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This probably considered necroposting but, I don't think one should let personal experience get on the way of an otherwise good article.

item.title: conflicted here
item.content:encoded:
As a furry I don't know whether to be deeply disturbed or owoffended.

Disturbed since I have known people that are extremely sadistic, attention-seeking, disconnected from reality, or all three, and I could see them acting like Ollie if they had the chance.

Owofended because I don't know if the author has a negative view on furries, some might take this seriously, and the "true family" thing does make sense for those with abusive or neglectful families that find meaningful relationships in the fandom, so it's far from anomalous

Either way this has unsettled me, like any good horror SCP. +1

Edit: have realized the author is a furry or at least supports them. I retract my anger. OwO on, brother/sister!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perry: Heh… Hewwo?

This scip is equal parts hilarious and terrifying and I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Way* late to the party, but just dropping by to say this is what made the article for me. Olliefox gets miffed that he's being called out and infects his 'friends' to shut them up. That level of inability/unwillingness to accept responsibility and just facing zero consequences for it freaked me right out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An SCP furry. Jesus Christ that's funny as hell. It also kept my attention the whole time and I got GENUINELY interested. It did even get unerving.
The uwu was kinda cringy like it should, but it later became creepy once we saw the others effected. So congrats you creeped me out with furries in a non sexulized way. +1

What really interested me was Perry at the end. How he was asking for help, albiet in a cancerous way.
His face was warped, but he was shakin and called out for help to the guards.
It makes me wonder if maybe even in the later stages they do still have their humanity? Maybe they are aware of their situation and how fucked it is, but their mind is to brainwashed? That sounds even more terrifying, to be aware of what your doing, but brainwashed to act and think a different way and can't control how you talk and act. Kind of like being a zombie, but from a cringy fandom that wants to fuck animal costumes.

This also means that there has to be furries that work and operate within the foundation and I don't know how to feel about that…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But I don't wanna be a furry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My fursuit is in transit. :3

+1. You sick, sick man.

Also, is olliefox trying to kill all furries, or are they unironically the worst of furry fandom?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
owo what's this?
*sprays 3312 infected computer with a flamethrower.* NO MUSKY HUSKIES.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This somehow perfectly walked the tightrope between "disturbing" and "masterwork of dark comedy". The only emotions I could feel while reading this thing in the midst of my laughter were unrelenting disgust and disdain for the anomaly, you for writing it, and myself for enjoying it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is… abhorrent, it's meandering nastyness feels like gravel on my soul…
+1
I usually go for more high concept and strange skips but there's something nice about a skip that doesn't pretend to be more than it is, this is a very well done thing about something that was done badly with all the precision of a crime scene report yet the chat interludes add a dimension without detracting much from that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perry: Heh… Hewwo?

God. Fucking damn it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This does good. Fantastic good. Great even. It takes the "Child speak is not cute dude! It's creepy!" that you sometimes see in forums and online and dials it up to SCP levels of horror. I'm really glad this was written and I love just the pure havoc this could wreck. When the next giant canon of Cool War/Resurrection/any other large hub, I can gladly see this SCP throwing in a splash of horror and driving home just how scary living in a world like this is.

+1 (which makes this 150)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd suggest checking out the whole thing versus just a few screencaps to formulate your opinion; I can admit that the concept is very strange and silly at face value!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Refraining from voting for now, but just concept-wise I feel like this would work better as a -J. Idk, I might be in the minority here, I didn't like the vaporwave SCP much either (even though I'm a furry who loves vaporwave). I feel like…SCPs based on an existing concept without much in the way of transformation always feel weak at best to me, and lazy at worst. I did just skim some screencaps from a tweet though, so I'm going to refrain from judgment until I read the whole thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh hell, i think i'm infected. i'm screwed

November: Hello, Mister Perry? Are you home?

Perry: Heh… Hewwo?

christ

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Immensely offputting (in a good way) and very well executed, with the chat logs placed next to the descriptions of the stages showing the slow decline of everyone Ollie afflicted. I hate it viscerally because of how disturbed it made me feel, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This struck such a chord in me that I just couldn’t enjoy it.

-1 for the personal story that I would rather not disclose, but great concept, and I probably would have liked it if it wasn’t for my experiences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's possible that he was already such an advanced form of furry trash that he constantly occupies an advanced stage of 3312 infection, and as such isn't as weirded out by the other infected as an unexposed person would be

It mentions that actual furries are immune, and 3312 infectees don't seem to notice or care about other infectees' weird behavior, so maybe furries don't see 3312 behavior as being particularly unusual

MPerry kind of shoots that in the foot in the last chatlog, though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UwU I weawwy weawwy wuv this SCP

Pwus 1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you know who I freel sorry for with this one?

MC&D. No really. Think about it. They will set up a fake company to higher Olliefox and let him have his furrsuit while he works but Ollie ends up infecting all his coworkers after finding out they aren't actually furries.

That or they recrut at a con and have to explain to the one furry who works at the foundation what they are doing there.

Also as I am a furry hypnotist myself I would be happy to give advce to anyone wanting to make a tale about this.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
This is an extremely well paced and structured story; I’m glad it came together so well.

Enjoy the reading!

https://youtu.be/C6h-qmExniU

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what are "wet squelching noises"???
make me creepy +1

item.title: Great one
item.content:encoded:
Great work, but shouldn`t this olliefox get an "SCP-3312-A" or something?

The karate vigilante part really intriguing.

Still a great one,definitely +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I used the Google chrome OwO what's this? Extension on this 10/10 would read with that again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Among other issues I have with this article, the primary one is that I don't actually understand the clear-cut motivation of olliefox, even with the understanding that this is the Foundationverse and what's been said in the discussion thread, and I think that's because you've written him in such a way (and I know that this isn't the case but this is the impression I got after reading this) that he feels like a caricature of a person, not an actual character.

item.title: You're a monster.
item.content:encoded:
Why would you make this? Who hurt you?

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was so amazing seeing this go from sandbox to the page! Congrats and +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
animated shows produced by ██████ █████████,

A brony SCP..?

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: completely forgot, my mistake. won't happen again.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: updogg, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …also, unless you're non-American and I didn't notice (in which case, my b) , quotation marks always appear after a fullstop, not before it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: FYI, a line of dialogue in one of the chat logs wasn't visible, because you forgot to put a space after one of the > symbols. went ahead and fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The XOF to GAW's FOX
Which works even better when you consider GAW backwards is WAG

In all seriousness though, I do find Olliefox to be an interesting character; We've seen the "Reality Bender that starts to think of themselves as innocent and perfect/godlike" before, but something about this article made it really interesting and way spookier to me. I think it was the casual-ness with which he infected his "friends" despite knowing it resulted in… that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perry: Heh… Hewwo?

I was going to upvote long before I got to the video log, but this interaction between Perry and the Mobile Task Furries really stuck with me. The video log is a fantastic addendum to this creepy-as-fuck anomaly. Everything before it painted a very unsettling picture of what being affected by this anomaly entailed. Then we got an example, and it just pushed this above and beyond.

Excellently done, and a well-deserved +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's the year 2018, "hewwo" is finally officially introduced to the SCP Universe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MTF-Omicron-Omega-Omicron (oωo) "What's This?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I applaud you, Dog Teeth. This SCP is splendid. The chat logs are well written, absolutely hilarious, and horrifying. Excellent jobs on the stages here, too.

RIP Perry
